page: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 4 shared vCPUs mapped to AMD EPYC 9575F processors running at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 200 GB SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring instruction set extensions such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier. For instance, the server achieves a PassMark CPU score of 12350.42 and handles 12.13 million Redis SET operations per second. However, large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with medium-model prompt processing and text generation scoring in the bottom 25%.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a memory-optimized profile that is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory density without requiring dedicated CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation presents a trade-off for sustained, high-intensity multi-threaded compute tasks, but the high single-core performance and generous memory allocation make it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database caching, web serving, and lightweight background processing.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 KVM-based virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processors running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 200 GB SSD. The hardware profile delivers a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database tasks perform at average levels. Conversely, medium-model LLM inference represents a performance bottleneck. This configuration provides qualitative resource density for memory-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. It is well-suited for database caching, web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring a higher memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-32GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high single-core performance, balanced memory density, and bundled local SSD storage.

Bundled 200 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database execution speeds
